macro_summary: Friday’s session was shaped by two large forces: Trump’s comments on a possible Iran peace deal drove oil sharply lower (WTI -2.3% to $84.29) and sent the S&P 500 up 0.3%, while the FOMC’s decision next week looms after the ECB raised rates. The semis rally was choppy — AMD surged 4% on a Citi upgrade, but ASML fell 2.8% on restructuring news, and the broader SOXX index added only 0.9%. The 10-year yield hovered at 4.49% as traders weighed easing oil risks against still-high CPI. The VIX dropped 9.1% to 17.68, signaling risk appetite returning, but caution is warranted with 11 of 27 names hard-blocked on extreme technical extension.
commodities_note: WTI fell to $84.29 on peace talk hopes, now down 16.6% over the month; gold edged up 0.5% to $4,240. The dollar index was flat at 99.81. The 10-year Treasury yield ticked up to 4.49%.

singapore.summary: The Singapore bank cluster remains mixed: DBS extended and blocked at +6.7% above its 50-day, while OCBC and UOB hold near their 50-day averages with better entry setups. The structural NIM compression (US10Y at 4.49%, MAS SGD appreciation) lingers, but the recent oil-price drop marginally eases USD funding costs. OCBC’s relative earnings resilience and UOB’s discounted valuation are the differentiators.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally cautious — rate headwinds persist, but OCBC/UOB showing NIM resilience.
singapore.key_development: US10Y ticked down to 4.49%, still far above the 4.20% NIM relief threshold, though oil's decline softens the USD funding side.

semiconductors.summary: The semis cluster is split between extreme extension and broken-down names. Eleven of the 15 semis are hard-blocked on extension (AMD, INTC, ASML, MU, TSEM, 000660_KS, LITE, NOK, IFX_DE, AIXA_DE, SITM), while NVDA and CRWV languish below their 50-day averages. SOXX gained 0.9% but the underlying divergence is stark: the AI photography and foundry names are overbought, while the rest lack catalysts. The cluster's thesis is structurally bullish on AI capex, but tactically extended across all entry points.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish, tactically extended — no clean entries in the group.
semiconductors.key_development: AMD surged on Citi upgrade, but the cluster is dominated by extreme overbought readings; SK Hynix’s US listing plan boosted 000660_KS.

big_tech.summary: BigTech is in a holding pattern: AMZN, GOOG, and MSFT all sit below their 50-day averages with no compelling entry signals. PLTR remains buried with a distorted R:R. The capex cycle keeps the long-term thesis intact, but near-term catalysts are absent — the cluster needs a round of earnings or a macro shift to change the posture.
big_tech.thesis_status: Structurally mixed — capex confirmed, but tactical setups are poor.
big_tech.key_development: The ECB rate hike narrowed divergence, but BigTech showed no reaction; all names held their recent support zones.

interconnected.name: Coherent Corp.
interconnected.reason: NVDA’s matching $2B photonics investment makes COHR the LITE read-through; both are optical interconnect plays for AI data centers.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if LITE’s upcoming design wins validate the photonics thesis or after the June 25 MU earnings for memory-cost signals.

interconnected.name: Marvell Technology
interconnected.reason: Custom ASIC design wins (Amazon Trainium, Google Axion) directly compete with AVGO and read on the hyperscaler ASIC spend trajectory.
interconnected.entry_note: Revisit after AVGO’s next earnings for ASIC order-book confirmation; entry on 8-10% pullback from current levels.

interconnected.name: Cadence Design Systems
interconnected.reason: EDA software export restrictions to China (read-through to ASML/INTC decoupling) make CDNS a direct beneficiary of US-China tech chokepoints.
interconnected.entry_note: Becomes interesting if the China Indium Phosphide curbs tighten further and ASML drops materially, suggesting an EDA substitution play.

interconnected.name: Qualcomm
interconnected.reason: Snapdragon AI edge chips leverage Arm architecture, serving as an on-device AI inference play alongside NVDA’s data-center buildout.
interconnected.entry_note: Monitor for a breakout above $200 if the on-device AI narrative accelerates at WWDC or Computex.

active_risks: The Iran–US standoff over the Strait of Hormuz entered day 101; Trump’s peace deal rhetoric and rising tanker traffic have pushed WTI below $85, but the crisis is unresolved.
China’s ongoing export controls on indium phosphide (InP) threaten the photonics supply chain for AI datacenters, directly affecting LITE, COHR, and the broader photonics ecosystem.
The ECB raised rates to 2.25%, narrowing the policy divergence with the Fed ahead of next week’s FOMC—rate-hike bets are growing globally.
G7 summit dynamics around Chinese subsidized exports and the upcoming FOMC decision are key catalysts for the week ahead.
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil’s decline to $84.29 has eased immediate inflation fears, but the semis cluster remains extended and offers no entry; NVDA and CRWV are below 50-day averages. The Singapore banks benefit marginally from lower USD funding costs. The defense-oriented PLTR is still overvalued.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Trump called off new strikes and hinted at a peace deal; US forces downed two Iranian drones near Hormuz. ECB raised rates as expected. SK Hynix chose Nasdaq for its US listing.

base.description: Hormuz stalemate continues, oil hovers $80-90; FOMC holds rates next week. Semis remain overbought, no new entries; Singapore banks grind sideways. NVDA and CRWV bounce off lows but lack conviction.

optimistic.description: Peace deal materialises, oil drops below $80, inflation fears recede; VIX falls below 15. Semis consolidation begins, offering entry points on pullbacks — LITE and AVGO become actionable.

pessimistic.description: Iran retaliates, oil spikes back above $100; FOMC signals a rate hike in July. Semis sell off hard; all extended names crash toward their 200-day MAs. Singapore banks suffer NIM blow from USD spike.

wildcard.description: China imposes a full export ban on InP; photonics stocks (LITE, COHR) plunge, but the disruption creates a long-term moat for US-based manufacturers once resolved.

geopolitical.portfolio_action: Stay cash-heavy; no new entries until VIX drops below 15 and at least three semis names reset to their 50-day averages.

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Defensive — extreme extension blocks entry across semis, no bullish setups in BigTech.
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: 11 of 27 names are hard-blocked on technical extension. The biggest risk is a sudden reversal in oil or a hawkish FOMC next week that could crash extended positions. Singapore banks offer some relative safety but lack catalysts.

scheduled_tech_events.notes: Vera Rubin samples confirmed shipping; optics integration revealed in Rubin rack design. Blackwell Ultra → Rubin transition timeline given.
Taiwan AI infrastructure partnerships; CoWoS capacity commitments from TSMC; HBM4 roadmap. Direct thesis-confirming event for AI Power Infrastructure cluster.
AI kit, on-device model integration, Vision Pro 2 rumours. Competitive signal for MSFT Copilot and GOOG Gemini on-device.

macro_trigger_map.bullish_outcome: Fed holds rates and signals no hike, cites easing oil prices; dovish tone lifts tech multiples.
macro_trigger_map.bearish_outcome: Fed signals a rate hike possible in July due to persistent inflation; yields spike, semis sell off.

macro_trigger_map.bullish_outcome: Strong HBM demand, guide raise; memory ASP cycle accelerates.
macro_trigger_map.bearish_outcome: Guidance miss, HBM2E prices soften; CAPEX caution from Goldman materialises.

first_pass_critical_violations: macro_trigger_map schema violation: expected a list of dicts each containing 'event', 'date', and at least one of 'bullish_outcome'/'bearish_outcome'. Malformed entries: [0] is a string: 'FOMC Rate Decision'; [1] is a string: '2026-06-18'; [2] is a string: 'Fed holds rates and signals no hikes, cites easing oil price'; [3] is a nested list (flatten failed); [4] is a string: 'Fed signals rate hike possible in July due to persistent inf' ... and 1 more. Regenerate with the correct structure or omit macro_trigger_map entirely.

taxonomy_discrimination.note: Output-only meta-analysis of signal-class discrimination. NOT injected into the analysis prompt and NOT persisted into memory.lessons_learned. full_corpus pools pre-cutover (different-instrument) rows — informational only.

calibration_insights.confidence_banner: Actionable buckets BUY (n=4) / ACCUMULATE (n=11) spanning chop, trend_up. NOT yet decision-grade — alpha figures are benchmark-relative; single-regime samples carry no out-of-regime evidence.
calibration_insights.note: Block is output-only — assembled post-LLM from signal_evaluation_log outcomes and never injected into the analysis prompt. The underlying lesson stats are separately persisted into report_memory.json under lessons_learned and DO reach the next day's prompt via the memory Layer 3 calibration loop. Treat as a reader-facing self-knowledge summary.
